_schema_meta.notes: Ground truth file for Gupta Prasad Satapathy Urolift claim. Curated manually from HDFC ERGO settlement letter. Powers V3.2 Settlement Advocate audit engine.

claim_header.ailment: Benign prostatic hyperplasia without lower urinary tract symptoms

post_settlement_balance.note: Protector Rider: 0 — explicitly confirmed by HDFC ERGO in settlement letter. Direct cause of ₹7,017 rider-dependent deductions.

reconciliation_totals.note: Grand total ₹4,63,900 verified against settlement letter. Asymmetry payload = insurer deductions (₹9,347) + hospital flags paid without audit (₹95,561.81).

line_items.flag_reason: Registration charges are non-medical administrative expenses. Not payable under any health insurance policy.
line_items.plain_english: Hospital charges ₹660 for registering your admission. This is their administrative overhead — not your liability under insurance.
Room and nursing charges correctly settled in full.

line_items.flag_reason: Annexure I consumable. Covered only if Protector Rider is active. Protector Rider: 0 on this policy.
line_items.plain_english: Under pads are a standard surgical consumable. HDFC ERGO deducted this because your policy does not have the Protector Rider active. If the rider were active, this ₹500 would have been paid.
Investigation charges correctly settled in full.

line_items.flag_reason: Annexure I consumable. Covered only if Protector Rider is active. Protector Rider: 0 on this policy.
line_items.plain_english: Ward material is a consumable item deducted because the Protector Rider is absent. Would have been paid with rider active.

line_items.flag_reason: Hospitality is a non-medical administrative charge. Not payable under any health insurance policy.
line_items.plain_english: Hospitality charges are not a medical expense. Not claimable under any scenario.

line_items.flag_reason: Annexure I consumable. Covered only if Protector Rider is active.
line_items.plain_english: Gauze is a surgical consumable deducted due to absent Protector Rider.

line_items.flag_reason: Annexure I consumable. Covered only if Protector Rider is active.
line_items.plain_english: Urine pot is a consumable deducted due to absent Protector Rider.

line_items.flag_reason: Annexure I consumable. Covered only if Protector Rider is active.
line_items.plain_english: Surgical clipper blade is a consumable deducted due to absent Protector Rider.

line_items.flag_reason: Annexure I consumable. Covered only if Protector Rider is active.
line_items.plain_english: Surgical gloves are consumables deducted due to absent Protector Rider.

line_items.flag_reason: Annexure I consumable. Covered only if Protector Rider is active.
line_items.plain_english: Examination gloves deducted due to absent Protector Rider.

line_items.flag_reason: Annexure I consumable. Covered only if Protector Rider is active.
line_items.plain_english: Surgical drape sheet deducted due to absent Protector Rider.

line_items.flag_reason: Annexure I consumable. Covered only if Protector Rider is active.
line_items.plain_english: Urine bag deducted due to absent Protector Rider.

line_items.flag_reason: Injection charges are classified as non-medical expenses by HDFC ERGO. Treated as part of nursing service — not separately payable.
line_items.plain_english: Injection charges are considered part of nursing care by the insurer. Not payable as a separate line item.

line_items.flag_reason: Annexure I consumable. Covered only if Protector Rider is active.
line_items.plain_english: Cotton is a consumable deducted due to absent Protector Rider.
Main OT charges including Urolift implants and surgical fees. Correctly settled in full.
Professional fees correctly settled in full.
Miscellaneous charges correctly settled.

line_items.flag_reason: Medical Record Department charges are administrative overhead. Providing medical records to the patient or insurer is the hospital's legal obligation — not a billable service.
line_items.plain_english: MRD charges are the hospital's cost of maintaining your records. They cannot legally bill you for this.

line_items.flag_reason: CSSD (Central Sterile Services Department) — sterilisation of surgical tools. Annexure I item. Covered only if Protector Rider is active.
line_items.plain_english: Sterilisation of surgical instruments is a large deduction (₹3,030) entirely due to absent Protector Rider. This alone makes the rider worth considering at renewal.

line_items.flag_reason: Attendant pass is a non-medical administrative charge. Not payable under any health insurance policy.
line_items.plain_english: The hospital charges for a pass to allow your family member to be with you. This is not a medical expense.
Pharmacy bill settled after MOU discount of ₹2,949.14 applied.
GST correctly settled.

hospital_flags.flag_reason: Dr. Sumanta Kumar Mishra billed for First Visit Urology on Day 1 (July 22) and again on Day 2 (July 23). A first visit cannot occur twice with the same doctor on consecutive days. This is a billing code duplication.
hospital_flags.plain_english: You were charged ₹1,200 for a 'first visit' by the same doctor on two consecutive days. A first visit can only happen once. The Day 2 charge is duplicate billing.
hospital_flags.evidence_required: Request consultation log showing date, time, and nature of both visits.

hospital_flags.item: Clinically Improbable IV Fluid Volume — 21 Litres Normal Saline
hospital_flags.flag_reason: Hospital billed 4 units of Normal Saline 3000ml (₹3,000) and 3 units of Normal Saline 3000ml (₹2,391.81) — totalling 7 units × 3000ml = 21 litres for a single 24-hour minimally invasive Urolift procedure. Clinical standard for post-operative IV fluid in a 24-hour stay is 2-3 litres maximum. 21 litres is physiologically improbable and indicative of bulk ward stock being billed to the patient.
hospital_flags.plain_english: You were billed for 21 litres of saline drip for a one-day stay. The clinical maximum for this procedure is 2-3 litres. Ask the hospital to produce the fluid administration chart showing when each bag was administered.
hospital_flags.evidence_required: Request fluid balance chart and nursing notes confirming actual IV fluid administered.

hospital_flags.flag_reason: Hospital billed ₹84,000 as a separate Equipment Charge alongside ₹3,00,000 for Urolift implants and ₹50,400 for Operation Theatre charges. The scope of this ₹84,000 charge is undefined — it is unclear what equipment it covers that is not already included in the implant cost or OT charges. This is classic unbundling — splitting a single service into multiple line items to inflate the total.
hospital_flags.plain_english: You paid ₹3,00,000 for the Urolift implants and ₹50,400 for the operation theatre. The hospital also charged ₹84,000 for 'equipment' without explaining what this covers. Ask them to itemise exactly what this ₹84,000 paid for.
hospital_flags.evidence_required: Request written itemisation of what the Equipment Charge covers and why it is separate from OT and implant charges.

hospital_flags.flag_reason: OT Monitoring Charges billed separately alongside OT charges. Standard OT charges include patient monitoring as part of the procedure. Billing monitoring as a separate line item is unbundling of a service already covered within the OT fee.
hospital_flags.plain_english: Monitoring a patient during surgery is part of what OT charges cover. The hospital billed this separately as an additional ₹1,820 charge on top of the OT fee.
hospital_flags.evidence_required: Request hospital billing policy on OT monitoring — confirm whether this is included in standard OT charges.

hospital_flags.flag_reason: Patient was charged ₹7,000 for AC Single room and ₹1,760 for Nursing Service Charges. Despite these room and nursing charges, the hospital added Hospitality (₹610), Ward Material (₹770), Registration (₹660), and Attendant Pass (₹160) — totalling ₹2,200 in overhead charges that duplicate services already covered by room rent.
hospital_flags.plain_english: You paid ₹7,000 for a room and ₹1,760 for nursing. The hospital then added ₹2,200 in extra charges for hospitality, ward material, registration, and an attendant pass — services that should be included in the room and nursing charges.
hospital_flags.evidence_required: Request hospital policy on what is included in room rent and nursing charges.

hospital_flags.flag_reason: A Dietician IP Consultation (₹950) was billed for a patient admitted for a Urolift procedure — a minimally invasive urological surgery for benign prostatic hyperplasia. A dietician has no active clinical role in prostate surgery. The same ₹950 dietician charge also appears on the Cystoscopy bill 5 days earlier (July 17), confirming this is a template charge applied automatically regardless of whether the service was rendered. Hospital subsequently sent a staff member for a 1-minute visit during the Urolift stay to create a paper trail.
hospital_flags.plain_english: You were charged ₹950 for a dietician consultation during a prostate surgery. A dietician plays no role in this procedure. The same charge appeared on your cystoscopy bill 5 days earlier. This is a template charge billed to every patient regardless of clinical need.
hospital_flags.evidence_required: Request signed dietician consultation note with time, clinical assessment, and dietary recommendation given. If only a brief visit occurred, contest the full specialist consultation fee.

layer_1_hospital_flags.note: All six flags were paid by HDFC ERGO without audit. This represents insurer leakage — failure to exercise fiduciary duty over the policyholder's sum insured.

asymmetry_payload.note: Visible loss = what the insurer deducted and the patient knows about. Invisible loss = what the hospital charged incorrectly and the insurer paid without auditing. Total asymmetry payload = ₹1,04,908.81.

simulation_mode.description: If Protector Rider were active, rider-dependent deductions would be paid
simulation_mode.note: Simulation mode treats Protector Rider as active and recalculates settlement. Hospital flags remain unchanged in simulation mode — they are hospital errors, not insurer errors.
